title: Bali 7-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Bali 7-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Bali, Indonesia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Tanah Lot Temple and Uluwatu Temple & Kecak Fire Dance. Prices from $1981 per person (estimate).
highlights: Private pool villa stay in Ubud
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Bali, Indonesia — Tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round (highs 29-32°C); wet season runs roughly November to March with afternoon downpours, dry season April to October is sunnier. April to October (dry season) offers sunny days and lower humidity, ideal for beaches, hiking and temple visits; July-August is peak crowds and pricing. This itinerary blends Tanah Lot Temple, Uluwatu Temple & Kecak Fire Dance, Kuta Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Tanah Lot Temple, Uluwatu Temple & Kecak Fire Dance, Kuta Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Bali
itinerary.description: Land at Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Tanah Lot Temple — Iconic sea temple perched on an offshore rock. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Bali: Uluwatu Temple & Kecak Fire Dance & Tegallalang Rice Terraces
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Uluwatu Temple & Kecak Fire Dance — Clifftop temple with evening traditional dance performance. In the afternoon, continue to Tegallalang Rice Terraces — Iconic stepped emerald rice paddies near Ubud. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Bali: Ubud Monkey Forest & Mount Batur sunrise trek
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Ubud Monkey Forest — Sacred sanctuary with hundreds of long-tailed macaques. In the afternoon, continue to Mount Batur sunrise trek — Guided pre-dawn volcano hike with summit sunrise views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Nusa Penida island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Nusa Penida island, a popular side trip from Bali. Return to Bali in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Nusa Penida island

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Sidemen Valley rice fields — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Bali: Tirta Empul Water Temple & Waterbom Bali
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Tirta Empul Water Temple — Holy spring temple for ritual purification bathing. In the afternoon, continue to Waterbom Bali — Award-winning waterpark in Kuta. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Bali Swing Ubud or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to DPS for your departure flight.

travelTips: Hire a private driver for full-day sightseeing — it's affordable and removes the stress of navigating chaotic traffic.
Visit Tegallalang and Lempuyang early morning to avoid both crowds and midday heat.
Carry a stash of small IDR bills for temple donations, parking and street food.
Check Nyepi (Day of Silence) dates if traveling in March — the entire island shuts down, including the airport, for 24 hours.
Negotiate scooter/car rental prices and always wear a helmet — traffic enforcement and accident risk are real.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
A sarong for temple visits (some temples provide one, but bringing your own helps)
Reef-safe sunscreen and rash guard for surfing/snorkeling
A light rain jacket for wet season showers
thingsToDo: Sekumpul Waterfall viewpoint (small entry may apply)
Canggu rice fields and surf beaches
White water rafting on the Ayung River
Surfing lessons in Canggu or Kuta
thingsToAvoid: Renting a scooter without prior riding experience — Bali's traffic is chaotic and accidents among tourists are common.
Drinking tap water — stick to bottled or filtered water throughout the island.
Disrespecting temple dress codes — sarongs are mandatory at most sites, not optional.
The "closed road/temple" taxi scam redirecting you to a commission-paying shop — verify independently.
Swimming at Kuta or Uluwatu beaches during high surf without checking flag warnings — currents can be dangerous.
topRestaurants: Warung Babi Guling Ibu Oka — Balinese Roast Pork ($)
Naughty Nuri's Warung — BBQ Ribs / Indonesian ($$)
familyActivities: Turtle conservation visit at Serangan Island
adventureActivities: White water rafting on the Ayung River
Surfing lessons in Canggu or Kuta
luxuryExperiences: Private villa with an infinity pool in Ubud or Uluwatu
Fine dining tasting menu at Locavore
Private yacht day trip to Nusa Penida
Spa retreat at a jungle wellness resort
honeymoonExperiences: Private pool villa stay in Ubud
Sunset dinner at a clifftop Uluwatu restaurant
Couples spa and flower bath ritual
Private sunrise trek up Mount Batur together
budgetExperiences: Free surf and sunset at Kuta or Canggu Beach
Warung (local eatery) meals for a few dollars
Campuhan Ridge Walk for free jungle views
Ubud town temple and market wandering

festivalCalendar.name: Galungan & Kuningan
festivalCalendar.timing: Every 210 days per the Balinese pawukon calendar

localEtiquette: Dress modestly and wear a sarong/sash when entering Hindu temples.
Never touch someone's head, including children's — it's considered the most sacred body part.
Use your right hand for giving, receiving, and eating.
Step around, not over, offerings (canang sari) placed on the ground.
Remove shoes before entering homes and some temple areas.
localTransportGuide: Renting a scooter is the most common way to get around Bali's traffic, though hiring a private driver for the day is a safer, affordable option for longer trips between regions. Ride-hailing apps like Gojek and Grab work well in southern tourist hubs but are restricted in some traditional areas.
bestTimeToVisit: April to October (dry season) offers sunny days and lower humidity, ideal for beaches, hiking and temple visits; July-August is peak crowds and pricing.
weather: Tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round (highs 29-32°C); wet season runs roughly November to March with afternoon downpours, dry season April to October is sunnier.
visaInformation: Most nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens can obtain a Visa on Arrival (typically valid 30 days, extendable once) for a fee paid on entry. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~12.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Bali?
faqs.answer: Most nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens can obtain a Visa on Arrival (typically valid 30 days, extendable once) for a fee paid on entry. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Bali?
faqs.answer: April to October (dry season) offers sunny days and lower humidity, ideal for beaches, hiking and temple visits; July-August is peak crowds and pricing. Tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round (highs 29-32°C); wet season runs roughly November to March with afternoon downpours, dry season April to October is sunnier.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Bali safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Bali is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Bali?
faqs.answer: The local currency is IDR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Bali is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Private pool villa stay in Ubud experience.

reviews.author: Priya Botha
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Seminyak Beach in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Daniel Andersen
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Ubud Monkey Forest and Lempuyang Temple "Gate of Heaven" (small donation). Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ava Santos
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Jatiluwih Rice Terraces (UNESCO) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Jatiluwih Rice Terraces (UNESCO) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Anna Botha
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Bali is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Tanah Lot Temple. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
